composition_rules: Use native grid discipline as an invisible structure; the generated slide must not expose a generic equal-card grid.
Place the focal object on a deliberate grid span and keep support objects subordinate.
typography_rules: Use hierarchy to separate product promise, proof object, and support labels.
Avoid tiny implementation labels unless routed to trace.
spacing_rules: Use spacing, margin, and gutter tokens before module generation.
Whitespace must support focal scale instead of acting as decoration.
theme_rules: Do not copy Figma colors or event identity.
Generate an original theme policy with light and dark variants.
motion_or_interaction_rules: If interaction is referenced, convert it into ordered native reveal steps, not copied UI behavior.
anti_copy_rules: Do not copy source layouts, screenshots, brand marks, product UI, event graphics, or exact color systems.
benchmarks.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show precise alignment and focal hierarchy without source-brand resemblance.

composition_rules: Represent a presentation product as native editable slide surfaces, not raster screenshots.
Use native nested frames, mini-previews, and selected states only when they serve the workflow story.
typography_rules: Hierarchy must make the active slide and primary decision readable before support metadata.
Small labels are allowed only as trace-like annotations outside the main claim.
spacing_rules: Use spacing to separate active slide, surrounding context, and controls.
Do not let preview grids consume the same visual weight as the focal slide.
theme_rules: Keep the theme original and product-system neutral.
Use contrast to separate canvas, chrome, and content without copying source colors.
motion_or_interaction_rules: Translate interactions into native step sequences: select, focus, reveal, validate.
native_ppt_implications: Build mini-preview and active-surface modules from editable rectangles and text.
Trace benchmark_validation_probe for each slide using this reference.
anti_copy_rules: Do not copy source UI, deck templates, product screenshots, brand marks, or exact interface proportions.
benchmarks.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show a native editable slide-surface concept, not a screenshot of a presentation tool.

composition_rules: Compress product breadth into one native route diagram plus one hero demo object.
Do not use an equal-density feature matrix as the main composition.
typography_rules: Hierarchy must make the commercial shift readable before product names.
Use sparse support copy so demo action and business consequence dominate.
spacing_rules: Use spacing to isolate the demo path from secondary feature breadth.
Reserve a large quiet area for the proof object so finance details do not crowd it.
theme_rules: Use an original high-contrast commerce palette, not Stripe identity colors.
Keep financial trust cues restrained and functional.
motion_or_interaction_rules: Represent demo flow as a staged native reveal from trigger to transaction to result.
native_ppt_implications: Use editable route nodes, transaction markers, and callout text.
Trace commercial_usecase_id and workflow_decision_ids to prevent generic feature-grid output.
anti_copy_rules: Do not copy source layouts, screenshots, brand marks, transcript text, stage visuals, or exact Stripe product framing.
benchmarks.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show keynote-scale commerce proof without becoming a product catalogue.

composition_rules: Build toward a native climax object that proves the AI platform transformation.
Use supporting infrastructure scale as secondary context, not as a wall of announcements.
typography_rules: Hierarchy must separate demo result, enterprise impact, and infrastructure support.
Avoid dense model-name clusters unless routed into notes or trace.
spacing_rules: Use spacing to enlarge the climax object and push secondary announcements away from the focal field.
Keep proof routes readable at contact-sheet scale.
theme_rules: Create an original AI platform theme; do not copy Google Cloud identity, gradients, or event color systems.
Use material contrast to show platform depth without brand imitation.
motion_or_interaction_rules: Translate live demo rhythm into ordered reveal steps: pressure, action, result, scale.
native_ppt_implications: Use editable diagrams, native labels, and reveal-step metadata for the climax.
Trace benchmark_validation_probe and theme_validation_probe for every climax slide.
anti_copy_rules: Do not copy source layouts, screenshots, brand marks, keynote stage visuals, model announcement graphics, or source color identity.
benchmarks.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show one large AI transformation proof object with controlled supporting context.

composition_rules: Treat material behavior as a native layer that supports content, not as decorative glass effects.
Keep the proof object original and content-first with a clear functional layer above the slide field.
typography_rules: Hierarchy must keep content meaning ahead of material labels.
Use restrained type scale so the material system does not overpower the message.
spacing_rules: Use spacing to separate content, material layer, and control layer.
Avoid crowding translucent or layered shapes around text.
theme_rules: Create an original material-language theme with no Apple-like colors, UI shapes, or keynote identity.
Use light and dark variants only as generic material behavior tests.
motion_or_interaction_rules: Represent material adaptation as native reveal states, not copied video frames or Apple UI animations.
native_ppt_implications: Use editable translucent shapes, masks, and grouped layers where PPT supports them.
Trace source_brand_sanitization and theme_policy_id before public review.
anti_copy_rules: Do not copy Apple UI, screenshots, brand marks, device frames, Liquid Glass visuals, source prose, or keynote composition.
benchmarks.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show an original content-first material language, not Apple-like glassmorphism.

composition_rules: Every slide must pass a native glance test: one focal object, one headline, and one support path.
Use visual hierarchy to route detail away from the main slide when proof density rises.
typography_rules: Hierarchy must be visible through size, weight, contrast, and placement.
Avoid uniform label sizes that make a slide read as an engineering report.
spacing_rules: Use spacing to make the reading order unambiguous.
Group related elements with consistent internal spacing and separate unrelated groups with larger gaps.
theme_rules: Theme choices must support readability, not decoration.
Contrast must distinguish focal claim, proof object, and secondary annotations.
motion_or_interaction_rules: Use reveal order to preserve hierarchy instead of showing every proof element at once.
native_ppt_implications: Add editable grouping and layer-order checks to layout QA.
Trace typography_system_id and spacing_token_set_id for each slide.
anti_copy_rules: Do not copy tutorial diagrams, source screenshots, brand marks, course exercises, article prose, or template layouts.
benchmarks.qa_probe: At contact-sheet scale, a reviewer should understand each slide's first read without zooming.

composition_rules: Use native emphasis to make the most important data point carry the story.
Delete, route, or subordinate data that does not support the slide's decision.
typography_rules: Hierarchy must distinguish story headline, emphasized data, and explanatory note.
Use short declarative headlines instead of long source-like explanations.
spacing_rules: Use spacing to isolate the important data from supporting evidence.
Do not pack multiple unrelated charts into one slide.
theme_rules: Theme contrast should make the emphasized data feel intentional and original.
Avoid copying any Duarte visual system, course material, or webinar graphics.
motion_or_interaction_rules: Reveal the emphasized data after context so the viewer experiences the turn in the story.
native_ppt_implications: Use editable chart, diagram, or shape emphasis rather than image-only data.
Trace workflow_decision_ids when data is deleted, routed, or emphasized.
anti_copy_rules: Do not copy webinar visuals, course slides, source layouts, brand marks, full prose, transcripts, audio, video frames, or exercise files.
benchmarks.qa_probe: The contact sheet should show one emphasized data or proof point per relevant slide.
